changes_summary: Addressed FLAG-001: Step 3 no longer proposes parallelization. Changed from "replace serial loop with Promise.all()" to "add a comment explaining why the serial loop is required (RPC computes timeline_frame from MAX + 50 without locking)". The serial for...await loop stays as-is. Updated the corresponding success criterion from "uses Promise.all if RPCs are confirmed independent" to "has a comment explaining why serial insertion is required". Removed the question about RPC independence since the user already answered it. Updated assumptions to reflect the confirmed answer.

success_criteria.criterion: All 10 existing test files listed in Step 8 pass without modification (unless the test itself needs updating for the change)
No behavioral changes to selection, context menu, shot creation, or drag-drop from the user's perspective
No files added except potentially ClipContextMenu if extracted within ClipAction.tsx (same file)
Unused type aliases ShotGenerationPreview and ShotGenerationRow are removed
Inline arrow function props in GenerationsPaneGallery are replaced with direct references
createShotWithGenerationsPath serial loop has a comment explaining why parallel insertion is unsafe (RPC race on MAX + 50)
ClipAction.tsx context menu JSX is extracted into a named component (ClipContextMenu or similar) in the same file
hasDataTransferType in dragDrop.ts reduced from 4 fallback paths to 2
useModifierKeys avoids unnecessary re-renders when modifier state hasn't changed
AgentChat mergeSelectedClips has a comment explaining the generationId preference
ClipAction.tsx stays under 350 lines total (clip + extracted menu)
No new abstractions, helpers, or utility files created
Manual smoke test: select multiple gallery items, right-click, create shot, generate video — all work as before

assumptions: The two-panel selection model (gallery + timeline) is the stable architecture — no third selection surface is planned. registerPeerClear stays as-is. (User confirmed.)
Serial insertion in createShotWithGenerationsPath is required because the RPC computes timeline_frame from MAX(...) + 50 without row-level locking. Parallel calls would race. (User confirmed.)
Existing integration test coverage is sufficient for ClipAction extraction — no new test file needed. (User confirmed.)
useExternalDrop's 791-line file size is acceptable because the hook itself is small and the extracted functions are pure helpers.
SelectionContextMenu, useLassoSelection, and the GallerySelectionContext core logic are already clean and don't need changes beyond what's listed.
Cross-browser drag-drop compatibility for DOMStringList is no longer needed (Chrome < 72 is EOL).
